message:
feat(watchdog): tick 206 check_cx ping routes through list_panes_with_cx (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

MuxWatchdog::check_cx wrapped the WezTerm ping in timeout_with_cx(cx,
self.wezterm.list_panes()) — the outer timer bound to the caller's
cx, but the inner list_panes() future ran with ambient cx. Only the
timeout boundary observed caller cancellation; a cx-cancel fired
while list_panes was mid-RPC wouldn't cut the MuxPool connect/read
until the ambient-cx observation path caught up.

WeztermInterface has had list_panes_with_cx as a trait method since
ft-xbnl0.2.2 (wezterm.rs L58) with a default impl that delegates to
list_panes. Concrete impls override — e.g. WeztermClient routes
through MuxPool::list_panes_with_cx, which threads cx all the way
down to DirectMuxClient::list_panes_with_cx.

Tick 206 routes the ping through `self.wezterm.list_panes_with_cx(cx)`.
Now both the timeout boundary AND the inner future observe the
caller's cx. Impls without an override see trait-default delegation
(identical behavior to the legacy path); impls with an override get
end-to-end cx threading.

Note: the warning_probe at L623 is left on ambient
self.wezterm.watchdog_warnings() because no _with_cx sibling exists
on the trait for that method. Adding it would require a trait
extension across 5 implementations — out of scope for this tick.
The warning probe is still bounded by timeout_with_cx(cx, ...) so
an outer cx-cancel still lands at the timeout boundary.

Verification: 224 / 224 watchdog:: tests pass under
--features asupersync-runtime via fork bypass (CC=llvm clang,
CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-rusticmaple-target), including
mux_watchdog_detects_failure and the ping/warning contract pins.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -602,11 +602,20 @@ impl MuxWatchdog {
         let now = epoch_ms();
         let start = std::time::Instant::now();
 
+        // Tick 206 (ft-xbnl0.2.3): route the ping through
+        // list_panes_with_cx(cx) so the inner WeztermInterface call
+        // observes the caller's cx. Previously wrapped ambient
+        // list_panes() in timeout_with_cx — the outer timer bound
+        // the caller's cx but the inner future ran with ambient cx.
+        // Concrete impls like WeztermClient::list_panes_with_cx
+        // route through MuxPool::list_panes_with_cx for real
+        // cx threading; impls without an override see an identical
+        // trait-default delegation to ambient list_panes().
         let ping_ok = matches!(
             crate::runtime_compat::timeout_with_cx(
                 cx,
                 self.config.ping_timeout,
-                self.wezterm.list_panes()
+                self.wezterm.list_panes_with_cx(cx)
             )
             .await,
             Ok(Ok(_))
